title: skopeo inspect command - introduce a way to skip querying all available tags
body:
The commit in this PR introduces a new option to the `inspect` command which allows users to specify whether they want to disable querying and displaying all available tags for the image being inspected. This has been requested in https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/785

The new option is `--query-tags` which defaults to `true` (to preserve backward compatibility) and is an optional optional. When set to `--query-tags=false` the implementation skips querying the tags for the repository and the displayed output will contain an empty `RepoTags[]` property:

```
{
    "Name": "docker.io/library/python",
    "Digest": "sha256:5ca194a80ddff913ea49c8154f38da66a41d2b73028c5cf7e46bc3c1d6fda572",
    "RepoTags": [],
    ...
```

I couldn't find existing tests for this command so I haven't updated/added any. However, I've run a bunch of manual tests (with `--query-tags=false`, with `--query-tags`, with `--query-tags=true`, without `--query-tags`) and they all have gone fine.

Additionally, from a performance point of view, when not using or when not setting this to `false` and doing an inspect on a image which is expected to have a large number of tags (for example `docker.io/library/python`) results in a very noticable and big improvement. For example, the following command without this new option returns in around 15-16 seconds consistently (and displays all available tags):

```
time bin/skopeo inspect   --override-arch=amd64 --override-os=linux docker://docker.io/library/python
....
real	0m15.884s
user	0m0.173s
sys	0m0.131s
```
Whereas when run with `--query-tags=false` for the same image, it returns consistently in around 8-9 seconds (of course with `RepoTags: []`):

```
time bin/skopeo inspect --query-tags=false  --override-arch=amd64 --override-os=linux docker://docker.io/library/python
...

real    0m8.444s
user    0m0.153s
sys 0m0.120s
```

comments.body: I do not know if there is a precedence for this within skopeo, but is there a way to get the default value for the parameter from the environment so that I do not have to always remember to set the flag when calling it?

> I do not know if there is a precedence for this within skopeo, but is there a way to get the default value for the parameter from the environment so that I do not have to always remember to set the flag when calling it?

There are a few, pretty rare, environment variables like that, but they usually point at a different config file; not to globally change behavior of a tool.

It seems rather risky to me to have a global SKOPEO_INSPECT_NEVER_LIST_TAGS environment variable that gets inherited into various scripts, potentially silently breaking them.

Users in interactive environments can use shell aliases (which don’t affect scripts).

Here's a more drastic comparison against two of my repos in quay.io that contain a lot of tags:


Tags | `--query-tags` | Duration
---|---|---
8k | false | 0m1.273s
8k | true | 0m42.164s
377k | false | 0m2.422s
377k | true | 36m42.499s 

:rocket:

> @jaikiran it might be warranted to add a single-letter option to disable the tag listing, though (`-t`?`-n`? `-l`?) Absolutely non-blocking, it can be added any time later.

I decide to use `-l`. Actually, now that I'm typing this and notice that the lower case "l" (ell) looks like a upper case "I" (eye), I'm having second thoughts about it. I think "-t" is a better choice.

> > @jaikiran it might be warranted to add a single-letter option to disable the tag listing, though (`-t`?`-n`? `-l`?) Absolutely non-blocking, it can be added any time later.
> 
> I decide to use `-l`. Actually, now that I'm typing this and notice that the lower case "l" (ell) looks like a upper case "I" (eye), I'm having second thoughts about it. I think "-t" is a better choice.

PR has now been updated to use `-t` for this new option.

On second thought, this would have to be used as `--list-tags=false`, which is fair enough — but also `-t=false` (exactly like this, with the equals sign and no space), which is not nearly as convenient as I imagined. I’m afraid I didn’t realize this at first.

Could the flag be flipped, maybe to `-n`, `--no-tags`, so that users can just do `skopeo inspect -n docker://…`?

(What do others think?)

Hello @mtrmac,

> Could the flag be flipped, maybe to -n, --no-tags, so that users can just do skopeo inspect -n docker://…?

Yes that does look better. I've updated the PR to use these options now.
@jaikiran a few nits and suggestions, but great change!  TYVM for bringing it forward.

Hello @vrothberg ,

> I'd also love to have a simple test exercising the new flag similar to https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/systemtest/010-inspect.bats#L67.

Is that some internal (skopeo) specific test framework. It looks like it's bash shell driven? Is there some way I can trigger these tests locally?

Either way, I have added a new test which I think should work, but I haven't been able to test it locally given my lack of knowledge on how to run those tests. I guess we will know shortly how it goes in the CI run.

> Regarding the flag name: I would prefer `list-tags` or `query-tags` over `no-tags` since it feels more natural to turn a functionality off via `--do-something=false` rather than turning an off-switch on via `--do-not-do-something=true`.

I think the `--no-tags` is a bit more natural in this case because the current command by default lists those tags. So users who do want to disable that behaviour would be looking for "what do I pass to disable tags" and "pass --no-tags or -n" appears a more natural answer than "pass --list-tags=false or -l=false".

Having said that, I will let @mtrmac, you and others decide which one is better. I have let it stay `--no-tags` and `-n` for now in the latest PR. If there's an agreement to changing it to something else, please let me know and I'll go ahead and update the PR.

> Hello @vrothberg ,
> 
> > I'd also love to have a simple test exercising the new flag similar to https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/systemtest/010-inspect.bats#L67.
> 
> Is that some internal (skopeo) specific test framework. It looks like it's bash shell driven? Is there some way I can trigger these tests locally?

Yes, it's shell.  You can run them via `make test-system-local` or via `bats $testfile.bats`.

> > Hello @vrothberg ,
> > > I'd also love to have a simple test exercising the new flag similar to https://github.com/containers/skopeo/blob/main/systemtest/010-inspect.bats#L67.
> > 
> > 
> > Is that some internal (skopeo) specific test framework. It looks like it's bash shell driven? Is there some way I can trigger these tests locally?
> 
> Yes, it's shell. You can run them via `make test-system-local` or via `bats $testfile.bats`.

Thank you. Wasn't aware that "bats" is a general bash test framework. I installed it and tried using it on my local (macOS) system, but that failed with:

```
bats systemtest/010-inspect.bats 
 ✗ inspect: basic
   (from function `standard_setup' in file systemtest/helpers.bash, line 23,
    from function `setup' in file systemtest/helpers.bash, line 34,
    from function `bats_test_begin' in file /usr/local/Cellar/bats-core/1.2.1/lib/bats-core/test_functions.bash, line 85,
    in test file systemtest/010-inspect.bats, line 8)
     `@test "inspect: basic" {' failed
   mktemp: illegal option -- -
   usage: mktemp [-d] [-q] [-t prefix] [-u] template ...
          mktemp [-d] [-q] [-u] -t prefix
```
Looking at `helpers.bash` in skopeo repo, it uses `--tmpdir` option to `mktemp` which doesn't seem to be supported in the `mktemp` command on macOS. So I think that will need a separate fix.
The alternate `make test-system-local` fails too with a different error:

```
---> Making bundle: test-system
sed: -e: No such file or directory
make: *** [test-system-local] Error 1
```
I'll try and sort out these issues separately and if necessary raise a PR. For now, I'll let the CI jobs to show up any failures (one of which I just fixed and updated the PR just now).
I don’t think `make test-system` or `make test-system-local` is going to easily work in macOS: it needs `podman` to create and run containers. A best bet for local development would be a Linux VM.

> Regarding the flag name: I would prefer `list-tags` or `query-tags` over `no-tags` since it feels more natural to turn a functionality off via `--do-something=false` rather than turning an off-switch on via `--do-not-do-something=true`.

Both is widespread, e.g. `--verbose` vs. `--quiet`. (Maybe there’s a better option name we could use instead of `--no-tags`?)

Note that the Go (Cobra?) semantics is that `--no-tags=true` is the same as `--no-tags`, and `--no-tags true` is _not_ parsed as a value to the boolean option but as an unrelated argument, like `--no-tags=true true`. And the same is true for the short-form `-n`.

So one argument is that `--list-tags` is only ever used with `=false`, which is just extra typing for no user benefit. The reason I ended up asking for the flip to `--no-tags` is a stronger version of that, with Go parsing the short-form must be exactly `-t=false` (not `-t false`); single-letter options in Unix usually don’t use an equals sign; so that would be very unusual (and to a large extent defeat the point of having a short-option form.)

Given the current behavior of Go boolean options, it seems much more convenient to me to have options that opt in into a non-default behavior, whether or not they “enable” or “disable” functionality.

(Admittedly, we do have `--tls-verify=false`, but we can’t change that now — and in that case any impediment to users disabling TLS instead of fixing their setup is somewhat desirable 😜 ).

As we have more and more massive repos with many tags, I hope you consider making `--no-tags` behavior the default behavior. The `RepoTags` data could go behind an `--repo-tags` flag instead. I think that would be a lot clearer for users.

I get that there are some existing scripts that might rely on `RepoTags`, but there's been plenty of larger breaking changes in podman and skopeo over the years and it's ok. I think this one is worth it, and the man page can make it clear how to restore the behavior with `--repo-tags`.

> I get that there are some existing scripts that might rely on `RepoTags`, but there's been plenty of larger breaking changes in podman and skopeo over the years and it's ok.

I don‘t know about Podman. As for Skopeo, I’m sure it has happened, but I can’t remember a case where we have consciously accepted breakage. Did we break something notable without noticing?

> I think this one is worth it, and the man page can make it clear how to restore the behavior with `--repo-tags`.

In my view, a project can almost exclusively either be relied upon to keep scripts working, or it can’t; cases where it can break things without damaging the trust in stability do exist but are exceedingly rare.

In this case we have fairly easy alternatives (e.g. we could add a new subcommand `skopeo see` == `skopeo inspect -n`, probably with a better name than `see`; perhaps restructuring the data further, dropping the pointless name/tag values from the output) that don’t break compatibility, so it’s hard for me to see that this case is the right precedent for breaking compatibility.

But I know I’m a bit more focused on API stability than is usual, other maintainers might well have a different view.

I am personally not aware of breaking changes in Skopeo since at least 1.0.  If that happened, it was unintentional since the major version should, in theory, have been bumped.

Podman definitely had breaking changes which explains the 1.0 -> 2.0 -> 3.0 and early next year 4.0 version bumps.

This behavior _feels_ polarizing enough to potentially warrant is breaking change nature. What is the process to do a Major version bump to define the default behavior to not fetch tags? 

If this isn't the right time for a major version bump, can we make sure to keep this feature on the docket for a future major version bump?
I don't think that we need to skip the fetching the tags by default.  Opting out via this PR is a big improvement.  But that's just my 2 cents.

> What is the process to do a Major version bump to define the default behavior to not fetch tags?

Well there isn’t a precedent for Skopeo :) But I also don’t think it’s necessary.

---

> If this isn't the right time for a major version bump, can we make sure to keep this feature on the docket for a future major version bump?

That’s a good point. We don’t have a formal tracker, so I guess a `// TO DO: Flip the default in a future major version bump` would be the least we could do.
@vrothberg WDYT about the flag name / polarity? Should we call in more help to break the tie?

FWIW, quite a while ago in OpenShift 4 we were using `skopeo inspect` as part of the host OS upgrade process.  It turned out that because we heavily used tags for that image, and each node was calling `skoepo inspect` on upgrades, this at one point was the `#1` cause of quay.io database performance issues.  I guess quay.io wasn't doing any caching of tags, and perhaps still isn't.

https://github.com/openshift/pivot/commit/fb4271d9afc3cdbb5a0c7cc318e4547cd2876d19 for the curious.

> @vrothberg WDYT about the flag name / polarity? Should we call in more help to break the tie?

I am on board with the current naming :+1:
What about `--no-repo-tags` instead of `--no-tags`? "no tags" would imply that I don't care about tags at all, but I do care about one tag - the one I'm inspecting

Can we make it positive --repotags and then default to true, to keep current behavior, although I am fine with changing the default behaviour.

Not a big fan of the double negative --no-repotags=true|false. Verus --repotags=true|false.

> Can we make it positive --repotags and then default to true

@rhatdan to be explicit, do you think the bad ergonomics of https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/1480#issuecomment-941249761 is acceptable?

> What about `--no-repo-tags` instead of `--no-tags`?

\*shrug\* I have a strong opinion at all. Because there is a single-letter option, we can probably afford a more accurate but longer option; OTOH some people hate typing.

> "no tags" would imply that I don't care about tags at all, but I do care about one tag - the one I'm inspecting

Note that `skopeo inspect` is only providing a tag value in the `Tag` field on old schema1 images, and it’s either not reliable to be any particular tag value (if the registry doesn’t enforce tag matching), or it is exactly the tag used to write to the registry (if the registry does enforce it), i.e. something the caller has quite possibly provided on the command line — and the value is not present on newer images at all. I _can_ imagine processes where querying this schema1 field is important, in principle, but it seems increasingly unlikely now that default distribution/distribution configuration is to reject schema1.
Hello @mtrmac, I would like to take this to completion. What would you suggest we do to move this forward. At this moment, the name of the param seems to be one part of disagreement.
:+1: from my side, I am cool to merge
Thank you @mtrmac, @rhatdan, @vrothberg, @TomSweeneyRedHat and everyone else for reviewing, providing feedback and merging this.
A pleasure, thank you for contributing, @jaikiran !
